name: mistune

metadata.name: mistune
metadata.summary: A sane and fast Markdown parser with useful plugins and renderers

documentation:
# Mistune v3

A fast yet powerful Python Markdown parser with renderers and plugins.

## Overview

Convert Markdown to HTML with ease:

```python
import mistune
mistune.html(your_markdown_text)
```

## Useful Links

1. GitHub: <https://github.com/lepture/mistune>
2. Docs: <https://mistune.lepture.com/>

## License

Mistune is licensed under BSD. Please see LICENSE for licensing details.

classes.name: mistune.directives._rst.RSTDirective
documentation:
A RST style of directive syntax is inspired by reStructuredText.
The syntax is very powerful that you can define a lot of custom
features on your own. The syntax looks like:

```text
.. directive-type:: directive value
   :option-key: option value
   :option-key: option value

   content text here
```

To use `RSTDirective`, developers can add it into plugin list in
the {class}`Markdown` instance:

```python
import mistune
from mistune.directives import RSTDirective, Admonition

md = mistune.create_markdown(plugins=[
    # ...
    RSTDirective([Admonition()]),
])
```

functions.name: mistune.plugins.math.math
documentation:
A mistune plugin to support math. The syntax is used
by many markdown extensions:

```text
Block math is surrounded by $$:

$$
f(a)=f(b)
$$

Inline math is surrounded by `$`, such as $f(a)=f(b)$
```

```
param md

Markdown instance
```

functions.name: mistune.plugins.math.math_in_quote
documentation: Enable block math plugin in block quote.

functions.name: mistune.plugins.math.math_in_list
documentation: Enable block math plugin in list.

modules.name: mistune.directives.toc
documentation:
# TOC directive

The TOC directive syntax looks like:

```
.. toc:: Title
   :min-level: 1
   :max-level: 3
```

"Title", "min-level", and "max-level" option can be empty. "min-level"
and "max-level" are integers >= 1 and \<= 6, which define the allowed
heading levels writers want to include in the table of contents.

functions.name: mistune.plugins.abbr.abbr
documentation:
A mistune plugin to support abbreviations, spec defined at
<https://michelf.ca/projects/php-markdown/extra/#abbr>

Here is an example:

```text
The HTML specification
is maintained by the W3C.

*[HTML]: Hyper Text Markup Language
*[W3C]:  World Wide Web Consortium
```

It will be converted into HTML:

```html
The <abbr title="Hyper Text Markup Language">HTML</abbr> specification
is maintained by the <abbr title="World Wide Web Consortium">W3C</abbr>.
```

```
param md

Markdown instance
```

functions.name: mistune.plugins.speedup.speedup
documentation: Increase the speed of parsing paragraph and inline text.

functions.name: mistune.plugins.def_list.def_list
documentation:
A mistune plugin to support def list, spec defined at
<https://michelf.ca/projects/php-markdown/extra/#def-list>

Here is an example:

```text
Apple
:   Pomaceous fruit of plants of the genus Malus in
    the family Rosaceae.

Orange
:   The fruit of an evergreen tree of the genus Citrus.
```

It will be converted into HTML:

```html
<dl>
<dt>Apple</dt>
<dd>Pomaceous fruit of plants of the genus Malus in
the family Rosaceae.</dd>

<dt>Orange</dt>
<dd>The fruit of an evergreen tree of the genus Citrus.</dd>
</dl>
```

```
param md

Markdown instance
```

functions.name: mistune.plugins.footnotes.footnotes
documentation:
A mistune plugin to support footnotes, spec defined at Here is an example: ```text That's some text with a footnote.[^1] [^1]: And that's the footnote. ``` It will be converted into HTML: ```html

That's some text with a footnote.1

  1. And that's the footnote.↩

``` ``` param md Markdown instance ```

functions.name: mistune.plugins.ruby.ruby
documentation:
A mistune plugin to support `<ruby>` tag. The syntax is defined
at <https://lepture.com/en/2022/markdown-ruby-markup>:

```text
[漢字(ㄏㄢˋㄗˋ)]
[漢(ㄏㄢˋ)字(ㄗˋ)]

[漢字(ㄏㄢˋㄗˋ)][link]
[漢字(ㄏㄢˋㄗˋ)](/url "title")

[link]: /url "title"
```

```
param md

Markdown instance
```

functions.name: mistune.plugins.spoiler.spoiler
documentation:
A mistune plugin to support block and inline spoiler. The
syntax is inspired by stackexchange:

```text
Block level spoiler looks like block quote, but with `>!`:

>! this is spoiler
>!
>! the content will be hidden

Inline spoiler is surrounded by `>!` and `!<`, such as >! hide me !<.
```

```
param md

Markdown instance
```

classes.name: mistune.directives._fenced.FencedDirective
documentation:
A **fenced** style of directive looks like a fenced code block, it is
inspired by markdown-it-docutils. The syntax looks like:

````text
```{directive-type} title
:option-key: option value
:option-key: option value

content text here
```
````

To use `FencedDirective`, developers can add it into plugin list in
the {class}`Markdown` instance:

```python
import mistune
from mistune.directives import FencedDirective, Admonition

md = mistune.create_markdown(plugins=[
    # ...
    FencedDirective([Admonition()]),
])
```

FencedDirective is using >= 3 backticks or curly-brackets for the fenced
syntax. Developers can change it to other characters, e.g. colon:

```python
directive = FencedDirective([Admonition()], ':')
```

And then the directive syntax would look like:

```text
::::{note} Nesting directives
You can nest directives by ensuring the start and end fence matching
the length. For instance, in this example, the admonition is started
with 4 colons, then it should end with 4 colons.

You can nest another admonition with other length of colons except 4.

:::{tip} Longer outermost fence
It would be better that you put longer markers for the outer fence,
and shorter markers for the inner fence. In this example, we put 4
colons outsie, and 3 colons inside.
:::
::::
```

```
param plugins

list of directive plugins

param markers

characters to determine the fence, default is backtick
and curly-bracket
```

functions.name: mistune.plugins.task_lists.task_lists
documentation:
A mistune plugin to support task lists. Spec defined by
GitHub flavored Markdown and commonly used by many parsers:

```text
- [ ] unchecked task
- [x] checked task
```

```
param md

Markdown instance
```

documentation: Parse tokens for ordered and unordered list.

classes.name: mistune.markdown.Markdown

methods.name: mistune.markdown.Markdown.parse
documentation:
Parse and convert the given markdown string. If renderer is None,
the returned **result** will be parsed markdown tokens.

```
param s

markdown string

param state

instance of BlockState

returns

result, state
```

Markdown instance to convert markdown text into HTML or other formats.
Here is an example with the HTMLRenderer:

```
from mistune import HTMLRenderer

md = Markdown(renderer=HTMLRenderer(escape=False))
md('hello **world**')
```

```
param renderer

a renderer to convert parsed tokens

param block

block level syntax parser

param inline

inline level syntax parser

param plugins

mistune plugins to use
```

classes.name: mistune.core.BlockState

methods.name: mistune.core.BlockState.prepend_token
documentation: Insert token before the last token.

methods.name: mistune.core.BlockState.append_token
documentation: Add token to the end of token list.
The state to save block parser's cursor and tokens.

classes.name: mistune.core.InlineState

methods.name: mistune.core.InlineState.prepend_token
documentation: Insert token before the last token.

methods.name: mistune.core.InlineState.append_token
documentation: Add token to the end of token list.

methods.name: mistune.core.InlineState.copy
documentation: Create a copy of current state.
The state to save inline parser's tokens.

methods.name: mistune.core.Parser.register
documentation:
Register a new rule to parse the token. This method is usually used to
create a new plugin.

```
param name

name of the new grammar

param pattern

regex pattern in string

param func

the parsing function

param before

insert this rule before a built-in rule
```

methods.name: mistune.core.BaseRenderer.register
documentation: Register a render method for the named token. For example: def render_wiki(renderer, key, title): : return f'\\{title}\' renderer.register('wiki', render_wiki)

functions.name: mistune.plugins.table.table
documentation:
A mistune plugin to support table, spec defined at
<https://michelf.ca/projects/php-markdown/extra/#table>

Here is an example:

```text
First Header  | Second Header
------------- | -------------
Content Cell  | Content Cell
Content Cell  | Content Cell
```

```
param md

Markdown instance
```

functions.name: mistune.plugins.table.table_in_quote
documentation: Enable table plugin in block quotes.

functions.name: mistune.plugins.table.table_in_list
documentation: Enable table plugin in list.

classes.name: mistune.renderers.html.HTMLRenderer

methods.name: mistune.renderers.html.HTMLRenderer.safe_url
documentation:
Ensure the given URL is safe. This method is used for rendering
links, images, and etc.
A renderer for converting Markdown to HTML.

functions.name: mistune.plugins.formatting.strikethrough
documentation:
A mistune plugin to support strikethrough. Spec defined by
GitHub flavored Markdown and commonly used by many parsers:

```text
~~This was mistaken text~~
```

It will be converted into HTML:

```html
<del>This was mistaken text</del>
```

```
param md

Markdown instance
```

functions.name: mistune.plugins.formatting.mark
documentation:
A mistune plugin to add `<mark>` tag. Spec defined at
<https://facelessuser.github.io/pymdown-extensions/extensions/mark/>:

```text
==mark me== ==mark \=\= equal==
```

```
param md

Markdown instance
```

functions.name: mistune.plugins.formatting.insert
documentation:
A mistune plugin to add `<ins>` tag. Spec defined at
<https://facelessuser.github.io/pymdown-extensions/extensions/caret/#insert>:

```text
^^insert me^^
```

```
param md

Markdown instance
```

functions.name: mistune.plugins.formatting.superscript
documentation:
A mistune plugin to add `<sup>` tag. Spec defined at
<https://pandoc.org/MANUAL.html#superscripts-and-subscripts>:

```text
2^10^ is 1024.
```

```
param md

Markdown instance
```

functions.name: mistune.plugins.formatting.subscript
documentation:
A mistune plugin to add `<sub>` tag. Spec defined at
<https://pandoc.org/MANUAL.html#superscripts-and-subscripts>:

```text
H~2~O is a liquid.
```

```
param md

Markdown instance
```

modules.name: mistune
documentation:
mistune
~~~~~~~

A fast yet powerful Python Markdown parser with renderers and
plugins, compatible with sane CommonMark rules.

Documentation: https://mistune.lepture.com/

functions.name: mistune.create_markdown
documentation:
Create a Markdown instance based on the given condition. ``` param escape Boolean. If using html renderer, escape html. param hard_wrap Boolean. Break every new line into
. param renderer renderer instance, default is HTMLRenderer. param plugins List of plugins. ``` This method is used when you want to re-use a Markdown instance: ``` markdown = create_markdown( escape=False, hard_wrap=True, ) # re-use markdown function markdown('.... your text ...') ```

functions.name: mistune.util.escape
documentation:
Escape characters of `&<>`. If quote=True, `"` will be
converted to `"e;`.

functions.name: mistune.util.escape_url
documentation: Escape URL for safety.

functions.name: mistune.util.safe_entity
documentation: Escape characters for safety.

functions.name: mistune.util.unikey
documentation: Generate a unique key for links and footnotes.

functions.name: mistune.util.unescape
documentation:
Copy from `html.unescape`, but `_charref` is different. CommonMark
does not accept entity references without a trailing semicolon

classes.name: mistune.renderers.markdown.MarkdownRenderer
documentation: A renderer to re-format Markdown text.

functions.name: mistune.toc.add_toc_hook
documentation:
Add a hook to save toc items into `state.env`. This is
usually helpful for doc generator:

```
import mistune
from mistune.toc import add_toc_hook, render_toc_ul

md = mistune.create_markdown(...)
add_toc_hook(md)

html, state = md.parse(text)
toc_items = state.env['toc_items']
toc_html = render_toc_ul(toc_items)
```

```
param md

Markdown instance

param min_level

min heading level

param max_level

max heading level

param heading_id

a function to generate heading_id
```

functions.name: mistune.toc.render_toc_ul
documentation:
Render a \<ul> table of content HTML. The param "toc" should
be formatted into this structure:

```
[
  (level, id, text),
]
```

For example:

```
[
  (1, 'toc-intro', 'Introduction'),
  (2, 'toc-install', 'Install'),
  (2, 'toc-upgrade', 'Upgrade'),
  (1, 'toc-license', 'License'),
]
```

classes.name: mistune.renderers.rst.RSTRenderer
documentation: A renderer for converting Markdown to ReST.

methods.name: mistune.block_parser.BlockParser.parse_blank_line
documentation: Parse token for blank lines.

methods.name: mistune.block_parser.BlockParser.parse_thematic_break
documentation: Parse token for thematic break, e.g. `<hr>` tag in HTML.

methods.name: mistune.block_parser.BlockParser.parse_indent_code
documentation: Parse token for code block which is indented by 4 spaces.

methods.name: mistune.block_parser.BlockParser.parse_fenced_code
documentation:
Parse token for fenced code block. A fenced code block is started with
3 or more backtick(\`) or tilde(~).

An example of a fenced code block:

````markdown
```python
def markdown(text):
    return mistune.html(text)
```
````

methods.name: mistune.block_parser.BlockParser.parse_axt_heading
documentation:
Parse token for AXT heading. An AXT heading is started with 1 to 6
symbol of `#`.

methods.name: mistune.block_parser.BlockParser.parse_setex_heading
documentation:
Parse token for setex style heading. A setex heading syntax looks like:

```markdown
H1 title
========
```

methods.name: mistune.block_parser.BlockParser.parse_ref_link
documentation:
Parse link references and save the link information into `state.env`.

Here is an example of a link reference:

```markdown
a [link][example]

[example]: https://example.com "Optional title"
```

This method will save the link reference into `state.env` as:

```
state.env['ref_links']['example'] = {
    'url': 'https://example.com',
    'title': "Optional title",
}
```

methods.name: mistune.block_parser.BlockParser.extract_block_quote
documentation: Extract text and cursor end position of a block quote.

methods.name: mistune.block_parser.BlockParser.parse_block_quote
documentation:
Parse token for block quote. Here is an example of the syntax:

```markdown
> a block quote starts
> with right arrows
```

methods.name: mistune.block_parser.BlockParser.parse_list
documentation: Parse tokens for ordered and unordered list.
